# Local

> Same-browser identity, same-tab, no server.

Local is a browser-to-browser demo of three primitives shipped from
the sarwagya.wtf workshop:

- @0xsarwagya/ghost — persistent cryptographic identity without accounts
- @0xsarwagya/handoff — move application state between devices without a cloud
- @0xsarwagya/durable-local — named durable values in the browser

The app is a peer-to-peer chat that identifies its participants by
Ghost's non-extractable Ed25519 keys, bootstraps peers with a Handoff
artifact, and persists the conversation with durable-local. No server
sees the messages. No account is required.

## Primary pages

- [Local](https://local.sarwagya.wtf): the app

## Related

- https://oss.sarwagya.wtf/ghost — package landing + docs
- https://oss.sarwagya.wtf/handoff — package landing + docs
- https://oss.sarwagya.wtf/durable-local — package landing + docs
- https://github.com/0xsarwagya/local — the repo, MIT
